topSlowQueries.pattern: Large result set with heavy I/O — 26 GB read, 938 files after pruning (17,864 pruned = 95% pruning efficiency)
Extreme scan amplification — 6B rows read for 4,854 returned. Only 5 files pruned (0.2%) = no effective clustering benefit
Full table scan — 0 files pruned, all 5,459 files read. 1M row limit hit (PBI truncation). 16.5 GB read.

evidence.claim: PBI queries read 6 billion rows to return ~5,000 rows — scan amplification ratio 1,243,336:1
evidence.impactExplanation: 6B rows = full table scan of 6.07B row fact table. Execution takes 179s. Pruning ineffective (5/2736 files pruned = 0.2%).

evidence.claim: Clustering on dim_date_sk is not being utilised — PBI date filter goes through dim_date JOIN, not direct WHERE on fact table
evidence.impactExplanation: 0 files pruned out of 5,459 means Databricks cannot leverage liquid clustering. Direct date predicate on fact would enable pruning.

evidence.claim: Trade model generates 40,494 queries per day against the fact table alone
evidence.impactExplanation: 1.17M queries in 30 days at avg 0.85 GB each = ~1 PB of data scanned monthly from this single fact table.

evidence.claim: Peak hour (8 AM UTC) generates 82,350 queries per week with avg 2.05s each
evidence.impactExplanation: Warehouse concurrency is stressed at peak — 82K queries at avg 2s = 45.7 hours of compute in a single hour.

evidence.claim: Worst Trade query took 500s reading 26 GB and returning only 12,000 rows
evidence.impactExplanation: 8+ minutes for a single visual render. 99.6% of time in execution (499s). Pruning was effective (95%) but still 938 files to scan.

evidence.claim: 55,597 Trade queries exceeded 10 seconds in the last 30 days (4.7% of total)
evidence.impactExplanation: Queries >10s correspond to reading 1.1B+ rows on average, indicating full or near-full table scans.

evidence.claim: dim_product_option_single_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1 has the highest avg query duration at 12.1s (P95: 40s)
evidence.impactExplanation: Despite small row counts (890K avg), this UDA table participates in complex cross-table queries with the fact table.

evidence.claim: 3 bidirectional cross-filter relationships exist in the Trade model
evidence.impactExplanation: Bidirectional cross-filtering on DirectQuery tables generates additional subqueries and complex JOINs, increasing query cost.

evidence.claim: fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 is 116 GB with 6.07 billion rows across 2,760 files
evidence.impactExplanation: Large fact table with liquid clustering on dim_date_sk, dim_product_option_sk, _updated_date. Clustering works when direct predicates are applied.
